messages.content: True or False: Students should never write narrative reviews, as they don't have enough expertise or authority in their fields.

False. While students may not have the same level of expertise or authority in their fields as more experienced scholars or professionals, writing narrative reviews can still be a valuable learning experience. Narrative reviews allow students to deeply explore a topic, develop critical thinking and analytical skills, enhance their understanding of the literature, and improve their academic writing abilities. Additionally, by engaging with existing research, students can contribute fresh perspectives and insights, possibly identifying gaps in the literature that could inform future research. It's important for students to approach narrative reviews with humility and a keen awareness of the limits of their knowledge, while also recognizing the value of their contributions to academic discourse.
True or False: Submitting a grant has benefits even if you are unsuccessful in obtaining funding.

True. Submitting a grant proposal can have several benefits, even if the bid for funding is ultimately unsuccessful. The process of preparing a grant includes literature review, project planning, and budget formulation, all of which are valuable exercises that can sharpen research focus, clarify objectives, and help in identifying potential weaknesses or areas for improvement in a project. Additionally, the feedback received from grant reviewers can provide critical insights that could enhance the quality of future proposals and research projects. The experience also increases familiarity with the grant writing process, making subsequent applications potentially stronger and more competitive. Lastly, going through the process can help in establishing connections or collaborations with colleagues and mentors who can offer support, advice, and partnership for future projects.
True or False: Personal essays are a good place to address any gaps or weaknesses in your record.

True. Personal essays, especially those submitted as part of applications to educational programs or scholarships, can be an appropriate place to address any gaps or weaknesses in your academic or professional record. Doing so allows you to provide context, explain circumstances, or reflect on how you've grown from the experience. It's important, however, to focus on what you've learned or how you've improved, rather than simply making excuses. The key is to turn potential negatives into positives by demonstrating resilience, determination, and the ability to overcome challenges. This approach can help admissions committees or reviewers understand your journey better and evaluate your application more holistically.
True or False: Asking students to draft their own letters of recommendation is appropriate and ethical.

True, with qualifications. Asking students to draft their own letters of recommendation is a practice that can be found in both academic and professional settings, although it is somewhat controversial and opinions on its appropriateness and ethics vary.

From the perspective of those who consider it appropriate, this approach can have practical benefits. For instance, it can save time for the recommender, ensure that all relevant accomplishments are included, and give the student an opportunity to reflect on their achievements and goals. This practice is seen as particularly viable when the recommender has a good relationship with the student, plans to review and edit the draft to ensure it accurately reflects their views, and genuinely endorses the student’s application.

However, critics argue that this practice can put undue pressure on students, potentially compromising the authenticity and integrity of the recommendation process. They contend that recommendations should be entirely the work of the recommender to preserve their sincerity and objectivity.

Ethically, if this practice is adopted, transparency, consent, and honesty are crucial. The recommender should fully intend to support the student’s application and should thoroughly review, edit, and personalize the draft to ensure it genuinely reflects their opinions and observations. Importantly, all parties should understand that the final letter must be an honest and accurate representation of the recommender’s assessment of the student.

In summary, while drafting one's own letter of recommendation is practiced and can be managed ethically, it requires careful handling to ensure it serves the interests of both the student and the integrity of the recommendation process.
True or False: When writing grants, it is important to include a detailed timeline for the research, as this demonstrates feasibility.

True. Including a detailed timeline in a grant proposal is important as it demonstrates the feasibility of the research project. A well-constructed timeline indicates to reviewers and funding bodies that the applicant has carefully planned out the phases of their project, considered the realistic time required for each task, and is aware of potential deadlines and milestones. It shows that the applicant has a clear understanding of the project's scope and complexity, and is prepared to manage it efficiently. Additionally, a timeline helps in ensuring that the project aligns with the funding period and showcases the applicant's commitment to completing the project within the stipulated time. Thus, a detailed timeline can significantly strengthen a grant proposal by enhancing its credibility and appeal to funders.

A grant's background/significance section should address which of the following?


A. The importance of the research question


B. Gaps and limitations in previous research


C. How the proposed research will fill in the gaps and limitations in previous research.


D. All of the above

D. All of the above

The background/significance section of a grant proposal should address the importance of the research question, identify gaps and limitations in previous research, and explain how the proposed research will address these gaps and limitations. This section is crucial for establishing the context and justification for the proposed study, demonstrating its relevance, and highlighting its potential contributions to the field.

Which of the following statements represents a strong endorsement in a letter of recommendation?


A. She is a hard worker and a team player.


B. She is a good writer, who with guidance will develop her writing skills in English. She is also proficient in presenting at conferences.  


C. She is one of the most productive trainees that I've had the pleasure of working with in my 20 years at Dartmouth.


D. She puts forth her best effort in understanding not just the project but its purpose and background.

C. She is one of the most productive trainees that I've had the pleasure of working with in my 20 years at Dartmouth.

This statement represents a strong endorsement because it compares the individual favorably against a significant history of trainees, indicating that her performance is exceptional. It brings in the recommender's extensive experience as a benchmark, which adds weight to the praise. This comparative and specific endorsement is likely to leave a strong impression on the reader.

Which of the following statements expresses hesitation about a candidate?



A. He was generally punctual and respectful of our appointments. He had a long commute for his graduate studies and he was good at informing me when he would be late for our meetings. 


B. I have never had a former student demonstrate such ongoing commitment to a research effort.


C. Although he encountered difﬁculties in his research, he was never discouraged and was able to ﬁnd creative ways of troubleshooting and solving the problems.      


D. None of the above

A. He was generally punctual and respectful of our appointments. He had a long commute for his graduate studies and he was good at informing me when he would be late for our meetings.

This statement could imply a level of hesitation about the candidate's punctuality or time management skills, despite highlighting a positive attitude towards communication. The use of "generally" and the mention of needing to inform when being late could be interpreted as subtle reservations about the candidate's consistency in punctuality, which contrasts with the more unequivocally positive endorsements seen in the other options.

Which of the following excerpts best illustrates the concept of "show, don't tell."


A. In all of these contexts, he has amply  demonstrated  the confidence and creativity  that  will  make his  graduate path a thoroughly positive and successful one. 


B. She has the self-motivation, intelligence, and work ethic to be a successful researcher in a multidisciplinary scientific field.  


C. He is a very diligent student and research assistant, and is particularly well-liked by everyone because of his charming personality.


D. When he went to purchase a ticket, however, he not only purchased a ticket for himself, but he also purchased an additional ticket so he could take the young boy who was the live-in house help at the apartment where he was spending his spring break. It was an opportunity for the young boy to attend an event that he would never have otherwise experienced.

D. When he went to purchase a ticket, however, he not only purchased a ticket for himself, but he also purchased an additional ticket so he could take the young boy who was the live-in house help at the apartment where he was spending his spring break. It was an opportunity for the young boy to attend an event that he would never have otherwise experienced.

This excerpt best illustrates the concept of "show, don't tell" by providing a specific anecdote that demonstrates the individual's character through actions rather than simply stating their qualities. It enables the reader to infer kindness, generosity, and awareness of others' circumstances, making the description more vivid and impactful.
